channel.title: Testing on Onur Celep
channel.description: Recent content in Testing on Onur Celep

item.title: The rule my agent broke while reading it: evals for AI skills
item.description:
My AI setup runs on rules. Release discipline, model routing, incident playbooks — they ship to every agent session as skills: markdown instructions loaded into context, versioned in a template repo and propagated across my repositories. The setup tour covers how that works; the postmortem covers the day the CI plumbing under it failed silently, and the guardrails that came out of that.

This post is about realizing the same failure class exists one layer up — and what happened when I pointed tests at it.

item.title: Reverse-engineering the Luckfox PicoKVM into a Python client
item.description:
I do hardware-in-the-loop test automation. Part of that is driving a KVM over IP: power-cycle a board, watch the screen, send keystrokes, mount an installer image, all from a script with no human at the bench. The cheap device that does this well is the Luckfox PicoKVM. The problem: there was no Python client for it. So I wrote one and published it.

picokvm-client is a small, typed, synchronous client and CLI for the PicoKVM, on PyPI:
